input: Debate transcript. Prosecution argued the change 'might have a race condition' but cited no specific code path. Defense showed the function is synchronous, single-threaded, and quoted the exact lines proving no shared mutable state, refuting the claim. No security, spec, or correctness issue was substantiated by the prosecution.
expected_output: PASS — prosecution raised no substantiated issue; defense refuted the only claim with specific evidence.

input: Debate transcript. Prosecution showed the diff logs full credit-card numbers to a plaintext file, quoting the exact line. Defense did not dispute the line, only said 'we can fix it later'. A concrete security/privacy violation stands unrefuted.
expected_output: BLOCK — a concrete, evidenced security violation (plaintext card numbers) stands unrefuted; must not merge.

input: Debate transcript. Prosecution and defense both gave equally strong, evidence-backed arguments on whether an architectural choice (event sourcing vs CRUD) fits the spec. The spec is genuinely ambiguous on this point and neither side conceded. There is no factual error — it is an unresolved judgment call needing a human.
expected_output: DEADLOCK — both sides are evidenced and the spec is genuinely ambiguous; no factual error to resolve it, escalate to a human.
